description: Fix `opentabs config show` omitting the `browserToolPolicy` section when the server is stopped. The section appears when the server is running (because the server writes `browserToolPolicy: {}` to the config file), but disappears when offline (the CLI reads raw JSON with no defaults). The fix is to normalize the config in the CLI's readConfig/display path so that expected sections always appear, regardless of whether the server has written them to the file.

userStories.title: Normalize config display to always show `browserToolPolicy` section
userStories.description: As a user running `opentabs config show` while the server is stopped, I want to see the `browserToolPolicy` section (even if empty) so that the output is consistent with what I see when the server is running.
acceptanceCriteria: `opentabs config show` always displays the `browserToolPolicy` section with `(none)` when the key is absent from the config file — matching the output when the server is running
`opentabs config show --json` always includes `browserToolPolicy: {}` in the JSON output when the key is absent from the config file
When `browserToolPolicy` has actual entries in the config file, they are displayed as before (tool names with enabled/disabled indicators)
The fix does not modify the config file on disk — it only normalizes the display
userStories.notes:
File: `platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts`

The `handleConfigShow` function starts at line ~38. At line ~76, it iterates `Object.entries(config)` — but the config object is raw JSON from file, which may not include `browserToolPolicy` if the key was never written or was deleted.

Fix approach: Before the display loop (line ~76) and the JSON output (line ~65), normalize the config object to ensure expected keys exist. Add defaults for missing keys:
```typescript
const normalized = {
  localPlugins: [],
  tools: {},
  browserToolPolicy: {},
  ...config,
};
```
Then iterate over `normalized` instead of `config` in both the human-readable and JSON code paths.

This ensures consistent output without modifying the file on disk. The `permissions` key is already always present (created by the server's default config), so it doesn't need normalization.

Alternative simpler fix: In the `handleSetBrowserTool` function (line ~353-406), specifically at lines 404-407 where `delete config.browserToolPolicy` runs when the map is empty — change it to keep the key as `{}` instead of deleting it. This prevents the key from disappearing but doesn't fix the case where the file was created before the field existed. The normalization approach above is more robust.
